name: Grand Central Summer Pudding
recipeIngredient: 8 slices Italian-style country bread, crust removed
3 cups fresh or frozen unsweetened raspberries, (12 oz.)
4 cups fresh or frozen unsweetened blackberries, (16 oz.)
1/4 cup frozen cranberry or orange juice concentrate
Berries, sweetened whipped cream and fresh mint for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Spread the bread on a baking sheet and bake at 400°F until firm and dry but not toasted, about 5 to 8 minutes.
2. Arrange the bread around the sides and bottom of a 6-cup mold or bowl. Trim the bread to cover the mold completely, reserving the trimmings.
3. Combine the berries, sugar, juice concentrate, and liqueur in a heavy-bottomed saucepan over medium heat. Stir lightly to help the sugar dissolve while trying not to mash the berries.
4. Pour the fruit and liquid into the bread-lined mold. Place the reserved pieces of bread over the berries, putting the smaller pieces in the center.
5. Cover with a small plate and weigh down with a large can. Refrigerate overnight or for up to 2 days before serving.
6. To serve, unmold onto a plate and garnish with fresh berries, whipped cream, and mint.
